title: tilia dasystyla subsp. caucasica
intro: Tilia dasystyla subsp. caucasica, also known as Caucasian Silver Lime, is a subspecies of the Tilia dasystyla tree, native to the Caucasus region, which is characterized by its heart-shaped leaves and fragrant flowers. It has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and sedative properties, which are attributed to its essential oils, flavonoids, and terpenes. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing stress and anxiety, improving sleep quality, and soothing respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. Tilia dasystyla subsp. caucasica can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in aromatherapy to promote relaxation and calmness.
